claims.claim: SMOS is an OpenAI-compatible semantic memory proxy written in Rust.
Of 14 memory-for-AI projects analyzed, only 3 use proxy-based architecture.
claims.note: Research dated 2026-06-25. Recheck competitive landscape quarterly.

claims.claim: Mnemo has 1 star and is inactive (created Feb 2026, last updated Feb 2026).
claims.note: GitHub snapshot 2026-06-25. Star count and activity status may change.

claims.claim: SMOS is the only production-grade proxy-based memory system on the market.
claims.note: As of 2026-06-25. New entrants may appear. 'Production-grade' is a judgment based on test count, architecture, and feature completeness vs Mnemo/agent-cli.

claims.claim: Gap G1 (Memory IS the API) is unique to SMOS — nobody else positions this way.
sources: docs/research/seo-keyword-clusters-2026-06-25.md:143 (zero SERP results for 'memory proxy not tool')

claims.claim: Gap G2 (Multi-agent isolation) is under-served — r/AIMemory thread explicitly identified this gap.
Gap G3 (NLI contradiction detection) is unique — no competitor uses NLI.
sources: docs/research/smos-competitive-research-2026-06-25.md:128 (Price of Meaning paper validates NLI approach)

claims.claim: Gap G4 (Zero external dependencies) is unique — every serious competitor requires external DB.
The Price of Meaning paper (arXiv:2603.27116, MIT/Sentra, Mar 2026) mathematically proves vector-only retrieval degrades through interference.
claims.note: Paper claims retrieved from research; full proof not re-derived. Confidence 0.85 because the math is summarized, not re-verified.

claims.claim: MemoryOS paper (EMNLP 2025 Oral, arXiv:2506.06326) validates OS metaphor for memory management.
mem0 has 59,449 stars (snapshot 2026-06-25).
claims.note: Star count is a snapshot. Recheck on day of publication. Star count grows ~100-500/day for popular repos.

claims.claim: mem0 requires Qdrant or Postgres+pgvector plus an LLM API key.
sources: docs/research/smos-competitive-research-2026-06-25.md:15 (mem0 row: 'Qdrant / Postgres+pgvector / graph layer' + 'LLM key for extraction')

claims.claim: mem0 graph features are gated behind the $249/mo Pro tier.
claims.note: Pricing may change; verify on mem0.ai/pricing before publication.

claims.claim: mem0 raised $24M Series A.
Letta requires adopting its full agent runtime (framework lock-in).
Zep/Graphiti requires Neo4j (mandatory).
Cognee requires Neo4j + Postgres + vector DB + LLM key.
sources: docs/research/smos-competitive-research-2026-06-25.md:18 (Cognee row: 'Neo4j + Postgres + vector DB' + 'Neo4j + Postgres + LLM key')

claims.claim: Extraction runs off the request path, AFTER the client receives the response.
sources: docs/ARCHITECTURE.md:198 ('Stages 4, 5, 6 run off the request path')

claims.claim: Embedded SurrealDB (RocksDB + HNSW vector index).
Cosine similarity averages 0.82 between contradicting pairs in SMOS fixture corpus.
claims.note: The 0.82 figure is from the pre-v0.1.6 POC fixture corpus, cited in earlier .factcheck.json. Current README (v0.1.6+) does not contain this number in the body — verify it is still referenced in the README before quoting verbatim. If not, soften to 'cosine similarity does not reliably distinguish contradicting claims'.

claims.claim: 643 MB DeBERTa-v3 ONNX download on first start.
Confidence base 0.5, multi_source_bonus 0.2, no_contradiction_bonus 0.1, accept_threshold 0.7.
Seven failure modes all degrade gracefully (fail-open contract).
Drift-priority walk scans every candidate; first contradiction wins immediately.
Multi-persona isolation: memory_key is the namespace boundary.
Native DeBERTa NLI runtime detection: CUDA/DirectML/Metal/CPU without rebuild.
3-crate workspace with compile-enforced layering (smos-domain ← smos-application ← smos).
Git-backed memory sync: dual-write facts to git repo, re-hydrate via smos import-git.
SMOS unique phrasing 'memory is part of the API, not a tool the agent calls' has zero SERP results.
sources: docs/research/seo-keyword-clusters-2026-06-25.md:143 ('memory proxy not tool' — vol ~10, KD ~2, nothing exists)
claims.note: SERP snapshot 2026-06-25. SERPs change weekly. Verify on publication day.

claims.claim: Pain-point phrasings (e.g. 'every session starts from zero') come from real HN posts.
claims.note: Each phrasing is tied to a specific HN Show HN post via objectID in the SEO research.

claims.claim: Cognee's Show HN with generic 'memory layer' framing got 9 points.
Recommended Show HN title is ≤80 chars and uses no marketing words.
sources: marketing/strategies/smos.md §8 (Show HN title: 'Show HN: SMOS — OpenAI-compatible semantic memory proxy in Rust' = 67 chars)

claims.claim: 20 recommended GitHub topics are from SEO research Deliverable 4.
SMOS uses Rust 1.96, edition 2024, MIT license.

flags.claim: Cosine similarity averages 0.82 between contradicting pairs.
flags.action: Before quoting the 0.82 number verbatim in public-facing copy, verify it appears in the current README body (v0.1.6+). If not present, soften to 'cosine similarity does not reliably distinguish contradicting claims' and cite ARCHITECTURE.md:248 (drift-priority walk rationale) instead.

Star counts (mem0 59,449 / Letta 23,521 / Zep 27,922 / Cognee 22,279 / Hindsight 17,556) are 2026-06-25 snapshots. Recheck each via GitHub API on the day of publication. Update the comparison table accordingly.
Pricing may change. Verify on mem0.ai/pricing before publication.
